[Parent post]: I think we need to pull apart what “moat” means here. 

Is “moat” a protective infrastructure around your core value prop that defends your users & their experience from unethical and dangerous invaders, or is it a structural impediment to exploration for those inside so they’re unaware of better?

[Reply]: I think it could be either.

[Parent post]: Yeah — making money from, you know, building things that your users actually want to use!

It's revealing that we've reached the point where we assume it has to be moat-or-nothing. The industry that works that way can't die fast enough.

[Reply]: I think we need to pull apart what “moat” means here. 

Is “moat” a protective infrastructure around your core value prop that defends your users & their experience from unethical and dangerous invaders, or is it a structural impediment to exploration for those inside so they’re unaware of better?

[Parent post]: In case you hadn't put this together:

Your business only needs a moat if you intend to enshittify it.

Interesting perspective. Unsure if I agree, especially in the current age, though I see what you’re gesturing at.

[Parent post]: My apologies, and let me clarify: I don’t think you’re unaware of other social tech, at all. But I do find your perspective on the cultural impact of Threads curious, and I can acknowledge it was not the most artful way to communicate, “what the fuck? I do not understand this perspective at ALL”

[Reply]: Even that would’ve been better. So I apologize for minimizing your awareness of social media as a whole! That was unfair of me. 💜💜💜💜

[Parent post]: You're saying I'm in an "open social tech" bubble as if I don't use and/or observe/research anything else. That's not fair, Bryan, and I think you know that's not true about the way I work.

[Reply]: My apologies, and let me clarify: I don’t think you’re unaware of other social tech, at all. But I do find your perspective on the cultural impact of Threads curious, and I can acknowledge it was not the most artful way to communicate, “what the fuck? I do not understand this perspective at ALL”

[Parent post]: Except that’s largely by design, the same as how Instagram posts don’t break out into larger culture. And even when they do, they don’t get attributed to the platform. But there are millions of people on there who’d say they built their algorithm “brick by brick”. Which is a culture!

[Reply]: (As a note, Anuj, I’ve been analyzing & watching the movements/impacts of social media since about 2002 - I don’t write about it or have a business around it, but that doesn’t mean that I don’t have nearly 25 years of hard-earned insights and wisdom about it - Anil and I first met in 2007, I think.)

[Parent post]: I’m not treating you as ignorant. I’m really simply pointing out that it seems a bit wild to claim that something has no cultural impact when more people create memes and transmit them with each other on Threads in a day than do in Bsky in a month.

[Reply]: Im challenging your definition of “culture” as missing a large swath of cultural communication and development happening every day on Threads if you say it has “zero cultural impact.”

[Parent post]: Bryan, with all due respect, I've been on Threads analysis since day one of it's existence. Please don't treat me as ignorant of who uses it and how little impact it has outside of its user bubble.

[Reply]: I’m not treating you as ignorant. I’m really simply pointing out that it seems a bit wild to claim that something has no cultural impact when more people create memes and transmit them with each other on Threads in a day than do in Bsky in a month.
